_comment: Akamai Bot Manager configuration. Detects and categorizes bots into: HUMAN, BROWSER_BOT, GOOD_BOT, BAD_BOT. The backend reads Akamai-Bot-Score (0-100) via backend/akamai_middleware.py and rejects high scores on /api/v1/auth/*. See docs/AKAMAI_DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE.md.

detectionMechanisms.comments: JS challenge defeats simple curl-based credential stuffing. Real browsers solve it transparently.
Show CAPTCHA after 3 failed logins — balances UX and security.
Persistent device ID across sessions — catches credential stuffing across IP rotation.
JA3/JA4 fingerprinting — detects curl/python-requests impersonating browsers.
Akamai ML model detects anomalous request patterns (rapid navigation, no pauses).

botCategories.name: BROWSER_BOT
botCategories.comments: Browser extensions performing automated actions on behalf of user (e.g., password managers).

whitelist.name: Googlebot
whitelist.comments: If bazspark.com has marketing pages — currently no SEO content, can disable.

whitelist.name: HF Space healthcheck
whitelist.comments: Allow HF Space internal healthcheck to bypass bot detection.

botCategories.name: SUSPICIOUS_BOT
botCategories.comments: Show CAPTCHA — let the user prove they're human.

botCategories.name: BAD_BOT
botCategories.comments: Block outright — known malicious bots, scrapers, credential stuffers.

headers.name: Akamai-Bot-Score
headers.comments: Integer 0-100 — backend/akamai_middleware.py reads this and rejects >30 on auth endpoints.

headers.name: Akamai-Bot-Category
headers.comments: HUMAN / BROWSER_BOT / GOOD_BOT / SUSPICIOUS_BOT / BAD_BOT — for logging.

headers.name: Akamai-Bot-Preview
headers.comments: JSON blob with detection signals — for debugging only, do NOT log to DB.

credentialStuffing.comments: 50 distinct usernames from same IP in 5 min = credential stuffing attack.

distributedAttack.comments: 500 failed logins across multiple IPs in 10 min = coordinated attack.
